segments.text: The burden against the Wilderness of the Sea. As whirlwinds in the South pass through, So it comes from the desert, from a terrible land.
A distressing vision is declared to me;
The treacherous dealer deals treacherously, And the plunderer plunders. Go up, O Elam! Besiege, O Media! All its sighing I have made to cease.
segments.speaker: Unknown Prophet

segments.text: Therefore my loins are filled with pain; Pangs have taken hold of me, like the pangs of a woman in labor.
I was distressed when I heard it; I was dismayed when I saw it.
segments.speaker: Unknown Prophet

segments.text: My heart wavered, fearfulness frightened me; The night for which I longed He turned into fear for me.
Prepare the table, Set a watchman in the tower, Eat and drink. Arise, you princes, Anoint the shield!
For thus has the Lord said to me:
Go, set a watchman, Let him declare what he sees.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And he saw a chariot with a pair of horsemen, A chariot of donkeys, and a chariot of camels, And he listened earnestly with great care.
A lion, my Lord! I stand continually on the watchtower in the daytime; I have sat at my post every night.
segments.speaker: Watchman

segments.text: And look, here comes a chariot of men with a pair of horsemen!
Babylon is fallen, is fallen! And all the carved images of her gods He has broken to the ground.
segments.speaker: Watchman

segments.text: Oh, my threshing and the grain of my floor! That which I have heard from the Lord of hosts, The God of Israel, I have declared to you.
segments.speaker: Unknown Prophet

segments.text: The burden against Dumah. He calls to me out of Seir,
Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?
segments.speaker: Unknown Voice from Seir

segments.text: The morning comes, and also the night. If you will inquire, inquire; Return! Come back!
segments.speaker: Watchman

segments.text: The burden against Arabia. In the forest in Arabia you will lodge, O you traveling companies of Dedanites.
O inhabitants of the land of Tema, Bring water to him who is thirsty; With their bread they met him who fled.
For they fled from the swords, from the drawn sword, From the bent bow, and from the distress of war.
For thus the Lord has said to me:
Within a year, according to the year of a hired man, all the glory of Kedar will fail;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: and the remainder of the number of archers, the mighty men of the people of Kedar, will be diminished; for the Lord God of Israel has spoken it.
segments.speaker: The Lord
